result: FY2024 is 370-day (53-week) transitional year post-VMware acquisition (closed Nov 2023)
notes: Q1 FY2024 was 97 days. Revenue $35.8B->.6B (+44%) includes ~$16B VMware contribution. Cost of Revenue nearly doubled ($11.1B->$19.1B) due to VMware software amortization. Direct YoY comparison to FY2023 or peers is misleading.

result: Q3 FY2024 = -$1,875M; Q1 FY2024 = $1,325M (vs normal ~$3-5B/quarter)
notes: Large VMware integration charges, acquired intangible amortization, and restructuring costs depress net income throughout FY2024. FY2024 total $5.9B vs FY2023 $14.1B and FY2025 $23.1B.

result: FY2024 OpInc $13.5B despite Revenue +44% — margin compressed from 45% to 26%
notes: VMware-related amortization of acquired intangibles (~$8-10B/year) significantly impacts operating income. Margins recover in FY2025 (40%) as amortization declines.

result: Total Debt jumped from $40.8B (FY2023) to $69.8B (FY2024 Q1) due to VMware acquisition financing
notes: Broadcom issued ~$30B in new debt to fund the $69B VMware acquisition. Debt has been gradually reduced: $69.8B -> $67.1B by FY2025.

result: CapEx $452-623M/year — Broadcom is fabless (like QCOM/AMD/NVDA) but VMware software adds minimal CapEx
notes: CapEx is comparable to other fabless peers. Unlike Intel IDM model.
